title: Infant and Childcare Subsidies
text:
Infant and Childcare Subsidies - SupportGoWhere
Infant and Childcare Subsidies
Share link
Provides tiered subsidies to help cover infant care and childcare fees for Singapore Citizen children from different income backgrounds.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Working applicants: Basic Subsidy of $600 for infant care and $300 for childcare
Non-working applicants: Basic Subsidy of $150 for infant care and childcare
Additional Subsidy for working applicants
Collapse all sections
About this support

Working applicants (mother or single father who works at least 56 hours per month)

Basic Subsidy of $600 for infant care and $300 for childcare

Additional Subsidy of up to $710 for infant care and up to $467 for childcare

Non-working applicants

Basic Subsidy of $150 for infant care and childcare

Eligible applicants who are not working or who are non-parent caregivers can apply for Special Approval to receive the higher Basic Subsidy and Additional Subsidy that working applicants receive

Who is this for?

Types of subsidies

	

Eligibility criteria




For all subsidies

	

Child must be a Singapore Citizen

Enrolled in an infant care or childcare centre licensed by ECDA




Additional Subsidy

	

Working applicants (mother or single father who works at least 56 hours per month)

Monthly household income of up to $12,000, or monthly household income per capita of up to $3,000 for larger families with 5 or more family members in the same household

From 1 January 2027, monthly household income thresholds will be raised to $15,000, or $3,400 per capita




Special Approval

	

Main subsidy applicants who are not working due to the following reasons:

Looking for a job

Studying, training, or on course for at least 56 hours a month

Pregnancy

Medically unfit for work due to hospitalisation, long-term illness and/or permanent disability

Taking care of a special needs child

Taking care of a sick family member who requires a full-time caregiver

Caring full-time for a younger child aged 24 months and below who is not enrolled in a preschool

Incarcerated

Non-parent caregivers who are caring for the child such as grandparents, legal guardian, foster parents, and head of a children’s home

Families who are tenants of a rental flat under the HDB’s Public Rental Scheme or are beneficiaries of ComCare Short-To-Medium-Term Assistance (SMTA) or Long-Term-Assistance (LTA) will automatically qualify for maximum preschool subsidies upon application for Special Approval

How to apply?

Refer to Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) website to apply or make changes to subsidies

Where can I find help?

View more details

ECDA hotline: 6735 9213

Submit an enquiry

ECDA office
51 Cuppage Road, 
#08-01, 
Singapore 229469
